relations.note: Sets the enforced size floor over the scale definition.
Line-height minimum applies alongside the type scale.
Specifies the email CSS realization of the Nunito Sans body/heading family.

quotes: **Agenda** (Adobe font)
**Nunito Sans** (Google font), bolder weights, Title Case
| Body copy | **Nunito Sans** | **Nunito Sans** | **Aptos** |
*Agenda may be used as live text in digital for the campaign headline only*
Available Agenda weights: Light, Regular, Medium, Semibold, Bold.
Available Nunito Sans weights: Light, Regular, SemiBold, Bold, ExtraBold.
Available Aptos weights: Regular, Bold.

rules.intent: Enforce approved brand fonts per production medium with defined fallbacks.
rules.rule_text: Typography families are assigned by medium: Headlines/headers use Agenda in print (headline.font = Agenda) and Nunito Sans in digital/email (with bolder weights, Title Case), falling back to Aptos in Word/PPT/Excel. Body copy and footers/fine print use Nunito Sans across print and digital (Aptos fallback). Agenda may be used as live text in digital only for the campaign headline. Available weights: Agenda (Light, Regular, Medium, Semibold, Bold), Nunito Sans (Light, Regular, SemiBold, Bold, ExtraBold), Aptos (Regular, Bold).

quotes: `'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif`
Nunito Sans must be loaded via `@font-face`/Google Fonts link where the client supports it; the stack guarantees graceful fallback in Outlook and other clients that strip webfonts.

rules.intent: Guarantee legible fallback typography across email clients.
rules.rule_text: [GENERAL] Email uses the CSS font stack body.email.font_stack = 'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif. Nunito Sans must be loaded via @font-face / Google Fonts link where the client supports it; the stack guarantees graceful fallback in Outlook and other clients that strip webfonts.

quotes: H1 (section hero headline): 28px / 36px (≈130%), Nunito Sans ExtraBold or Bold
H2 (section heading): 22px / 30px (≈136%), Nunito Sans Bold
Body: 16px / 24px (150%), Nunito Sans Regular
**[GENERAL] Email type scale (px / line-height):**
Secondary/supporting body: 14px / 21px (150%), Nunito Sans Regular — use sparingly, never for primary content
Footnote/reference text within a section: 12px / 18px (150%) — must be "noticeably smaller" than primary data and clearly separated from it

rules.intent: Establish the consistent email type scale and hierarchy roles.
rules.rule_text: [GENERAL] Email type scale binds each level to its token: H1 (h1.type = 28px/36px, Nunito Sans ExtraBold or Bold), H2 (h2.type = 22px/30px, Bold), H3 (h3.type = 18px/26px, Bold or SemiBold), Body (body.type = 16px/24px, Regular), Secondary/supporting body (secondary_body.type = 14px/21px — use sparingly, never for primary content), and Footnote/reference text (footnote.type = 12px/18px — must be noticeably smaller than primary data and clearly separated from it).

quotes: **16px is the brand-mandated minimum for body copy ("bigger is better")**
16px is the brand-mandated minimum for body copy
Never set running text below 12px. (ISI sizing is owned by the locked component.)

rules.intent: Guarantee legibility floor for body and running text.
rules.rule_text: 16px is the brand-mandated minimum for body copy ("bigger is better"), matching body.type = 16px. Never set running text below 12px. (ISI sizing is owned by the locked component.)

quotes: **Line height: minimum 150% for all body copy** (brand accessibility rule)
**For light text on dark backgrounds, increase further** — [GENERAL] use 160% (e.g., 16px/26px).

rules.intent: Maintain readable line spacing, with extra leading for light-on-dark text.
rules.rule_text: Line height is a minimum of 150% for all body copy (body.line_height = 150%, brand accessibility rule). For light text on dark backgrounds, increase further — [GENERAL] use 160% (e.g., 16px/26px).

quotes: Never use two adjacent text blocks at the same size+weight to signal different hierarchy levels.

rules.intent: Preserve legible reading order through distinct hierarchy levels.
rules.rule_text: Use a clear type scale / visual hierarchy so the reader knows what to read first, next, last. Never use two adjacent text blocks at the same size+weight to signal different hierarchy levels.
